mappings.rationaleAI: Hinduism from the Indus Valley relates to Indian civilization (A), Indus civilization (B.2), caste system (B.3.b), South Indian development (B.7.b), and later developments (C, D.3).
Indian philosophical traditions connect to Indian civilization (A), Brahmanical development (B.3), Buddhism (B.4.a), and later cultural developments (D.3).
The historical Buddha directly addresses the early development of Buddhism (B.4.a).
Buddhism's origins connect to pre-Mauryan states (B.4.a). Theravada relates to Ceylon's conversion (E.1).
Alexander's conquest directly addresses the invasion of India (B.4.b).
Trade routes relate to South Indian maritime trade with the West (B.7.a).
Archaeological methods connect to sources (A) and prehistoric settlement (B.1, B.2).
Provides context on the caste system and Indian political traditions that originated in the period covered by this section.
Dynastic rule connects to Mauryan empire (B.5), Gupta dynasty (C.1), and Rajput kingdoms (D.1).
